bids.reasoning: I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid at the very start of negotiation, since there is no real time pressure at t=0 despite the opponent’s urgency message. The opponent’s first offer is valid but gives me low utility overall, mainly conceding only on issueD and issueE, so I keep my high-weight priorities while matching their issueD valueB, which is equally best for me.

At t=0.02 it is still very early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making only a small concession from my prior offer to avoid simply repeating it. The opponent has repeated the same offer and their urgency messages are not supported by the actual early time, so I treat them as cheap talk; their bid suggests they may value issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, which I can accommodate without cost.

I am targeting a near-maximum utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.04) and the opponent has not conceded; their latest offer actually worsened for me by moving issueD from valueB to valueC. Their urgency messages are cheap talk given the real time fraction, so I am holding firm on high-weight issues while choosing issueD=valueB and issueE=valueA, which they have already shown some willingness to offer.

I am targeting a near-maximal utility bid because it is still very early (t=0.06) and the opponent has not conceded; their recent offers remain very low for my utility and even worsened on issueD. Their urgency messages are cheap talk and inconsistent with the actual early time, so I will not make major concessions yet, but I avoid repeating my exact previous bid by switching issueD to an equally valuable option for me.

I am targeting a very high utility bid (about 0.98) because it is still very early at t=0.08, so there is no need to make large concessions yet. The opponent’s offers remain poor for me, mostly holding issueA/issueB/issueC/issueF at valueC, with only issueD varying; their urgency messages are cheap talk and not supported by the actual early time fraction. I make a small concession on issueA while holding firm on the other high-value issues and matching their latest acceptable issueD value.

At t=0.10 it is still early, so I am targeting a very high-utility bid while making a small concession on the lower-weight issueF rather than on the more important issues. The opponent’s offers have mostly held firm on issueA/B/C/F and their urgency messages are cheap talk; their only meaningful movement improved issueD, which is already acceptable to me at valueA or valueB.

I am targeting a very high-utility bid around 0.964, since it is still early at t=0.14 and there is no need to make major concessions yet.
